title: Suicide mode d'emploi
description: A handbook discussing methods of suicide that became central to a French legal crackdown.
descriptionParagraphs: A handbook discussing methods of suicide that became central to a French legal crackdown.

Suicide mode d'emploi is organized less as a story than as an argument. As a instructional manual, it tries to persuade readers through selection, emphasis, and direct claims about suicide, instruction, and public health.

Its significance lies in the way it compresses large claims into memorable formulas and positions. Even readers who reject the work usually have to reckon with how sharply it frames questions about suicide, instruction, and public health.

overview.englishDescription: A handbook discussing methods of suicide that became central to a French legal crackdown.
banningParagraphs: Suicide mode d'emploi entered censorship debates as a instructional manual associated with suicide, instruction, and public health. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around self harm and instructional harm.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1987 onward in France, where French lawmakers and courts made reprints illegal. The book's notoriety helped produce legislation against provocation to suicide. Here the legal target was not blasphemy or indecency but a perceived public-health danger.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Suicide prevention
counterReadings.author: World Health Organization
counterReadings.note: Public-health counterpoint for instruction manuals centered on self-harm or assisted death.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: Included here as a civic counterpoint when bans blur personal autonomy, panic, and state control.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
